title: Carmakers Defeat Most Allegations in UK Dieselgate Class Action
summary: Car manufacturers including Mercedes-Benz Group AG, Ford Motor Co. and Renault SA largely won a case over allegations they manipulated vehicles to cheat on emissions tests, after a London court rejected most of the claims in the closely-watched class action case.

title: The Memo That Killed the Penny
summary: Previously undisclosed documents obtained by FOIA Files reveal how the Trump administration found a way to halt penny production and why officials considered the decision “critical.”

title: What Happens When a $20 Billion Data Center Meets a Tornado?
summary: Data center campuses are becoming so large they’re straining traditional insurance models. And they’re increasingly clustered in areas prone to destructive weather.

title: Stocks Open Higher, SK Hynix to Debut, Trump on Housing, More
summary: Listen for the latest from Bloomberg News

title: Trump Says Ceasefire With Iran Is Over
summary: President Donald Trump said the US would continue talks with Iran but considers the ceasefire between the countries to be over. James Jeffrey, former US ambassador to Iraq and Turkey, talks about Trump's objectives in Iran and why the US is targeting infrastructure. He speaks on "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Won’t Sign Housing Bill But Will Allow It to Become Law
summary: President Donald Trump said he would not sign a bipartisan housing bill in protest over lawmakers declining to approve a separate voter-ID bill he has prioritized. Bloomberg's Tyler Kendall joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aluminum Declines as EGA Restarts Key Alumina Refinery in UAE
summary: Aluminum fell after Emirates Global Aluminium said it restarted a key alumina plant in Abu Dhabi that was attacked in the early stages of the Iran war.

title: Stocks Eke Out Gains as SK Hynix Rallies in Debut: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders drove stocks mildly higher in the countdown to the earnings season, with SK Hynix Inc. jumping in its debut and the market parsing the latest geopolitical developments.

title: Nasdaq Says SK Hynix Bonanza Opens Path to More Foreign Listings
summary: Nasdaq Inc. President Nelson Griggs said SK Hynix Inc.’s blockbuster listing is spurring other international companies to consider the US for either initial public offerings or the sales of American depositary receipts.

title: BHP Explores Sale of Chilean Desalination Plant, Power Lines
summary: BHP Group is exploring the sale of a desalination plant in Chile, along with its electricity transmission assets, extending a strategy of monetizing infrastructure as the world’s biggest miner concentrates capital on its core copper business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Wheat Soars as Ukraine Attacks on Russia Seen as Export Risk
summary: Wheat prices jumped Friday as traders weighed potential shipping disruptions following Ukraine’s attacks on top exporter Russia.

title: SK Chairman Promises ‘Much, Much Bigger’ US Investment Plan
summary: SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won, fresh off a landmark US stock offering for the conglomerate’s memory-chip business, said he has an ambitious plan to invest more money in America.

title: Sports Bet Pays Off as Omers Reaps Nearly 60% Gain on MLSE
summary: Ontario’s pension fund for government workers generated a return of close to 60% in less than three years on a bet tied to Toronto’s major league sports teams, vindicating an investment that was initially viewed as pricey.

title: Oil Slumps as US, Iran Continue Talks After Renewed Fighting
summary: Oil slumped as the US and Iran appear set to continue talks following a flare-up in attacks that strained their ceasefire and deterred tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Stocks Waver as SK Hynix Makes US Debut | Open Interest 7/10/2026
summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." SK Hynix makes Wall Street history with the biggest-ever U.S. debut by a foreign company. Delta beats earnings expectations, but shares fluctuate on a cloudy outlook. And earnings season ramps up next week, with Wall Street's biggest banks set to report another blockbuster quarter. Emily Roland of Manulife John Hancock Investments, John Stoltzfus of Oppenheimer Asset Management, and Di Zhou of Thornburg Investment Management weigh in as traders hold off on making big bets ahead of the weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Traders Hedge for FX Volatility Return Amid Fed Uncertainty
summary: Currency traders are starting to buy protection against bigger exchange-rate swings after months of calm, as banks warn that shifting Federal Reserve expectations and elevated geopolitical tensions could be about to jolt markets.

title: Sports Investing Made Accessible for Everyone
summary: Marques Colston, former wide receiver for the New Orleans Saints, discusses the focus of his private sports venture 'The Champion Fund,' and his efforts to make sports investing accessible to all. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The Stock Market and a “Phenomena of our Lifetimes”
summary: Something big has changed in the makeup of the US economy.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 7/10/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Delta Travel Demand Withstands Fuel Costs
summary: Delta’s earnings beat and it reaffirmed its full-year profit guidance as travel demand withstands higher prices. Sheila Kahyaoglu, Senior Equity Research Analyst at Jefferies, discusses the outlook for the airline industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stock Futures Slip After Tech Rally, SK Hynix Starts Trading
summary: US equities were trading mixed Friday as investors digested the record US listing of South Korea’s SK Hynix Inc. and prepared for the start of second-quarter earnings season.

title: JPMorgan Builds AI Agents That Beat 60/40 Model in Backtests
summary: As investors increasingly turn to artificial intelligence for help with everything from stock picking to risk management, JPMorgan Chase & Co. has been testing whether a model can do something more ambitious: allocate money itself.

title: SK Hynix Open to Issuing More US Shares, SK Group Chairman Says
summary: SK Hynix Inc. is open to issuing more US shares should returns prove strong and the company’s stock price remains stable, SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said.

title: CazéTV, febre da Copa, é alvo crescente de escrutínio por bets
summary: Giovanna Serafim conta como a CazéTV está cada vez mais no foco dos órgãos reguladores no Brasil por publicidade de apostas para jovens durante a Copa. Edição de Patricia Xavier e produção de Barbara Nascimento e Gabriel Diniz Tavares.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Delta Rises on Profit Guidance; EasyJet Moves on Apollo Bid | Stock Movers
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers:

- Delta (DAL) shares are higher after it reaffirmed its full-year profit guidance, citing strong demand for premium, corporate and international travel that helped offset the highest quarterly fuel expense in its history. The airline earned an adjusted $1.56 a share in the second quarter, topping analysts’ estimates, with revenue rising 14% from a year earlier and capacity increasing just 1%.
- EasyJet (EZJ) shares are moving on news it received a fresh offer from Apollo Global Management for 715 pence a share, beating a rival proposal from Castlelake. EasyJet is "no longer minded to recommend the Castlelake proposal" and would be minded to recommend Apollo's bid to its shareholders, given that Apollo's £5.7 billion bid is superior to Castlelake's £5.5 billion offer.
- WD-40 (WDFC) shares jumped after the lubricant spray maker boosted its net sales forecast for the full year. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EasyJet Gets £5.7 Billion Apollo Offer in Possible Bidding War
summary: EasyJet Plc received an offer from private equity firm Apollo Global Management for 715 pence a share that beats a rival proposal from Castlelake LP, a surprise twist in the takeover saga that sets up a possible bidding war between the two US investment funds.

title: Europeans Are Changing Saving Habits to Invest More, ING Says
summary: Europeans are showing a durable shift in savings habits toward products such as investment funds that might one day help support greater spending, ING economists said.

title: Americans Are Stuck in a Rut — And a Lack of Fun Might Be to Blame
summary:
America appears to be suffering from a fun shortage. From the rising cost of concert tickets, to bowling alley and nightclub closures, to venue shortages for kids to play in local sports leagues, the fun deficit is real and it's making Americans more isolated and lonelier than previous generations.

Bloomberg's Ben Steverman joins Stacey Vanek Smith and Max Chafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to explore some easy solutions to bring fun back into our everyday lives. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Heads for Weekly Loss as Traders Consider Iran, Rates Path
summary: Gold headed for a weekly loss as investors assessed renewed fighting in the Middle East and the prospects for interest-rate hikes to combat inflation.

title: Cencosud Shares Dive as Strategic Plan Yet to Boost Earnings
summary: More than a year into a new strategic plan at Chile’s Cencosud SA, the retailer’s shares have slumped and the company is asking investors for patience.

title: Morgan Stanley Sees Pressure Rising on Chipmakers’ Pricing Power
summary: Morgan Stanley’s Lisa Shalett urged investors to be cautious on chipmakers as evidence mounts that their pricing power is limited, suggesting that the stocks may have rallied too far on optimism around artificial-intelligence spending.
